head.title: SQL Island - English
head.teaser: The english version of the classic game "SQL Island" adapted to work in Eskuel Suite.

text-scene.text: During the flight, something strange happens. A glowing portal suddenly appears in the sky, and the plane is pulled straight into it. For a moment, everything goes dark.

When the plane finally lands, nothing looks familiar. There are no towns, no roads, and no modern tools.
Instead, you see forests, wild animals, and a small tribe living near a cave.

select-scene.text: "You arrive at the prehistoric village 'Stonehaven'. Have a look at the villageid of the prehistoric village ;
"Before doing anything else, first you should meet the people living here. Look at all the members of Stonehaven."
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT * FROM Inhabitant WHERE villageid = ( SELECT villageid FROM Village WHERE name = 'Stonehaven' );
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT * FROM Inhabitant WHERE villageid = ( SELECT villageid FROM Village WHERE name = 'Stonehaven' );

manipulate-scene.text: Sqlaus realizes that these people do not belong to the modern villages. They are part of an ancient tribe. Use a CTE to identify all inhabitants of the Prehistoric Village and insert them into the TribeMember table. Do this all using a single query! (HINT: use the villageid in a WHERE clause)
manipulate-scene.sql-solution: WITH tribe AS ( SELECT personid, name, job, gender, age FROM Inhabitant WHERE villageid = 4 ) INSERT INTO TribeMember (name, role, gender, age) SELECT name, job, gender, age FROM tribe;

select-scene.text: Make sure that you have moved all the inhabitants of Stonehaven to the TribeMember table. You should see that there are all members in the TribeMember table.

text-scene.text: Near the campfire you meet Anuk, the hunter, and Lena, the gatherer. They tell you that the tribe is preparing a big hunt to catch the mammoth.

select-scene.text: Before the hunt begins, you should check what things the tribe currently has.
"The tribe has discovered fire. Fire allows new cooking techniques such as roasting. Find all foods that the tribe can cook using fire." HINT: You need 3 different tables
select-scene.sql-solution: SELECT f.name FROM Food f JOIN CookingMethod c ON f.methodid = c.methodid JOIN Thing t ON c.required_thing = t.thingid WHERE t.name = 'fire';
select-scene.sql-placeholder: SELECT f.name FROM ... f JOIN ... c ON f.methodid = c.methodid JOIN ... t ON c.required_thing = t.thingid WHERE t.name = 'fire';

select-scene.text: Anuk and Lena want to cook a mammoth steak for the whole tribe. To prepare the meal, they first need a spear to hunt a mammoth and use a knife to cut the meat. Before sending Anuk to hunt, check if they have both arms.

Good. Now we know we need to craft a knife and a spear. Crafting some tools requires other things first. For example, a knife needs a sharp stone, and a sharp stone needs stone. To follow these chains of requirements, we can use a recursive query. A recursive query repeatedly looks for the materials needed to craft a thing until it reaches the basic materials.
select-scene.sql-solution:
WITH RECURSIVE make_tool(thingid) AS (
SELECT thingid FROM Thing WHERE name = 'knife'
UNION
SELECT c.required_thing FROM Crafting c JOIN make_tool mt ON c.product_thing = mt.thingid )
SELECT 'knife -> ' || t.name FROM make_tool mt JOIN Thing t ON mt.thingid = t.thingid WHERE t.name <> 'knife';
select-scene.sql-placeholder:
WITH RECURSIVE make_tool(thingid) AS (
SELECT thingid FROM Thing WHERE name = '...'
UNION
SELECT c.required_thing FROM Crafting c JOIN make_tool mt ON c.product_thing = mt.thingid )
SELECT 'knife -> ' || t.name FROM make_tool mt JOIN Thing t ON mt.thingid = t.thingid WHERE t.name <> '...';

select-scene.text: Now try to find all the things required to craft a spear.
select-scene.sql-solution:
WITH RECURSIVE make_tool(thingid) AS (
SELECT thingid FROM Thing WHERE name = 'spear'
UNION
SELECT c.required_thing FROM Crafting c JOIN make_tool mt ON c.product_thing = mt.thingid )
SELECT 'spear -> ' || t.name FROM make_tool mt JOIN Thing t ON mt.thingid = t.thingid WHERE t.name <> 'spear';

select-scene.text: Well, done! Anuk went to collect all the raw materials, and now he must look for his team from the hierarchy table for who can shape the weapons and who supports him with resources and supplies. Together, they form the hunting team that will prepare for the challenges ahead.
select-scene.sql-solution: WITH hunting_team AS ( SELECT h.boss_id, h.worker_id, h.category FROM hierarchy h WHERE h.category = 'hunting' ) SELECT b.name AS boss, w.name AS worker, w.job AS worker_job, h.category FROM hunting_team h JOIN inhabitant b ON h.boss_id = b.personid JOIN inhabitant w ON h.worker_id = w.personid;
select-scene.sql-placeholder: WITH hunting_team AS ( SELECT h.boss_id, h.worker_id, h.category FROM hierarchy h WHERE h.category = 'hunting' ) SELECT b.name AS boss, w.name AS worker, w.job AS worker_job, h.category FROM hunting_team h JOIN inhabitant b ON h.boss_id = b.personid JOIN inhabitant w ON h.worker_id = w.personid;

select-scene.text: Great! Aruk handovers the raw materials to Bruk. The hunting team now has a new knife and a new spare. Update the things.

text-scene.text: Great! Anuk is ready for the hunt. Anuk takes the spear and walks toward a dark cave where the mammoth was last seen. He enters the cave to start the hunt...
"Anuk followed a mammoth into the caves… but he never returned. Lena found an old map showing how the caves are connected."

select-scene.text: Explore the map. HINT: there is a table with all the connections
You have to help Lena to find the path that leads to the mammoth room: "Starting from the entrance, explore all the paths to reach *mammoth_room*. Be careful: some tunnels form loops!"

text-scene.text: “Lena rushes toward the mammoth room. She hears heavy footsteps… and a familiar voice shouting for help. Anuk is cornered by the mammoth! He cannot defeat it alone — he needs tools, courage, and a plan.”

select-scene.text: “Lena sees Anuk struggling. The mammoth is too strong — they need a trap! But a mammoth trap is complex and Lena can't remember what things are needed to built it. Use a recursive CTE to help Lena gather all materials required to craft a mammoth trap.”

text-scene.text: “The trap snaps shut with a thunderous crash. The mammoth roars, stumbles, and collapses. Lena and Anuk stand frozen for a moment — then relief washes over them. Together, they harvest the precious meat and drag it back toward the village."

“After a while, Lena and Anuk arrive back at the village. The tribe celebrates the victory, but the elders insist on fairness. Every villager should receive food according to their needs and status. Lena must calculate how much meat each person deserves.”

select-scene.text:
Use a multi-step CTE to compute the total food demand for each tribe member.
**Rules**
Each tribe member’s food demand is calculated from four independent components:
1. Role-based demand • hunter → +4 • gatherer → +3 • toolmaker → +3 • cook → +2
2. Gender-based adjustment • m → +2 • f → +1 • other → +0
3. Age-based adjustment • Age 5–19 → +1 • Age 20–39 → +2 • Age 40–65 → +3
4. Hierarchy bonus • Each tribe member receives +1 for every subordinate they have in the table (i.e., count of rows where they appear as ).
**Goal**
Combine all these analyses using multiple CTEs and produce a final table showing: • personid • name • total_need ordered from highest to lowest total_need.
select-scene.sql-solution:
WITH role_demand AS ( SELECT personid, CASE role WHEN 'hunter' THEN 4 WHEN 'gatherer' THEN 3 WHEN 'cook' THEN 2 WHEN 'toolmaker' THEN 3 ELSE 2 END AS role_need FROM TribeMember ),
gender_demand AS ( SELECT personid, CASE gender WHEN 'm' THEN 2 WHEN 'f' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS gender_need FROM TribeMember ), age_demand AS ( SELECT personid, CASE WHEN age BETWEEN 1 AND 19 THEN 1 WHEN age BETWEEN 20 AND 39 THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS age_need FROM TribeMember ), hierarchy_bonus AS ( SELECT boss_id AS personid, COUNT(*) AS bonus FROM Hierarchy GROUP BY boss_id ), combined AS ( SELECT t.personid, t.name, r.role_need + g.gender_need + a.age_need + COALESCE(h.bonus, 0) AS total_need FROM TribeMember t JOIN role_demand r ON t.personid = r.personid JOIN gender_demand g ON t.personid = g.personid JOIN age_demand a ON t.personid = a.personid LEFT JOIN hierarchy_bonus h ON t.personid = h.personid ) SELECT * FROM combined ORDER BY total_need DESC;
select-scene.sql-placeholder:
WITH role_demand AS ( # Your code goes here... FROM TribeMember ),
gender_demand AS ( # Your code goes here... FROM TribeMember ), age_demand AS ( # Your code goes here... FROM TribeMember ), hierarchy_bonus AS ( # Your code goes here... FROM Hierarchy GROUP BY boss_id ), combined AS ( SELECT ... FROM TribeMember t JOIN ... JOIN ... JOIN ... LEFT JOIN ... ) SELECT ...

text-scene.text: The game is over. We hope you enjoyed it!
